what_it_does: A generated RG10 per-mockup result artifact for the A1-canvas-empty-desktop.html screen. Records the screen grounding (screen_present + plain-language what_this_is / what_you_can_do), run counts (n_candidates=28, n_dossiers=28, n_clusters=21), and two lists: confirmed (13 entries) and flagged (8). Each entry carries a dossier (address, capabilities, grounding, howto{what,what_you_can_do,how_to_change}, maps_to_feature, represents), its cluster_size/cluster_members, the confirm verdict (jury votes_grounded/votes_ungrounded/quorum/unanimous + reasons), and the source mockup filename.
observations: ground describes the empty canvas starting screen — drag a node from the palette or click Start Demo
Counts: 28 candidates → 28 dossiers → 21 clusters → 13 confirmed + 8 flagged (21 surfaced clusters)
Confirmed entries carry a unanimous-grounded jury (e.g. votes_grounded 3/3, quorum true, reasons [])
Flagged entries carry a no-quorum jury (e.g. 0/3 grounded) with a named soft-accuracy reason
Dossier addresses use ui:// scheme (e.g. ui://toolbar/layers); howto.how_to_change cites real source files (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx) and the build-intent wire
represents uses concept codes (LAY-layers) and maps_to_feature uses feature codes (NODE-layer)
standouts: Each element's dossier includes a how_to_change pointing at the actual code region and the build-intent wire — the element explains how to modify itself
The jury verdict object distinguishes votes_grounded vs votes_ungrounded with quorum/unanimous flags
Flagged reason explicitly framed as Layer-2 SOFT (variance not error)
conventions: Consistent dossier schema across confirmed and flagged entries
ui:// addressing scheme and concept/feature code conventions (LAY-, NODE-)
Generated-artifact shape consumed by rg10_surface.py / rg10_writeback_run.py (the sibling scripts read exactly these keys)
concerns: Generated data — no file-local concerns; correctness depends on the upstream chain, not visible here
notes: A generated registry-candidate result for the empty A1 canvas screen: 21 unique elements (13 confirmed, 8 flagged) each with an at-altitude dossier and a jury verdict — the per-mockup unit the surface/writeback scripts aggregate.
questions: What model/prompts produced the dossier prose and the jury votes (only counts are recorded here)?
Why 21 clusters but 13+8=21 surfaced — are all clusters surfaced as either confirmed or flagged?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — generated data artifact, no stated purpose of its own beyond being the run output.
apparent_themes: Self-describing UI elements at the operator's altitude (what / what-you-can-do / how-to-change)
Jury-based grounding confirmation with explicit flagging of non-quorum
Grounding UI claims in real source paths
intention_signals: An autonomous attempt to turn one rendered mockup's clickable elements into grounded, plain-language registry dossiers, with a voting jury separating confirmed from flagged so the operator only adjudicates the uncertain.
novelty: Dossiers that bind a UI address to both a plain-language capability description and a concrete how-to-change pointer into the codebase.
contribution: Contributes the A1-canvas-empty screen's grounded element dossiers and jury verdicts — 13 confirmed and 8 flagged ui:// entries with how-to-change pointers — as one consumable per-mockup registry-run record.
summary_for_embedding: Generated RG10 per-mockup artifact for the empty A1 canvas screen: 28 candidates clustered to 21, yielding 13 confirmed and 8 flagged ui:// element dossiers with jury verdicts and how-to-change source pointers.
intention_for_embedding: To capture one mockup screen's clickable elements as grounded self-describing registry dossiers with jury confirmation, for operator review and write-back into the address registry.
